events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 100,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Zero vs. span error pattern on calibration sheet indicates instrument drift or sensor degradation while in service",
    "Calibration sheet (FD+DOC) shows the instrument cannot maintain both zero and span within tolerance simultaneously, consistent with a degraded sensing element or transmitter electronics",
    "In-service instrument exhibiting calibration drift poses risk of inaccurate process measurement and potential control loop upset"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove instrument from service and isolate per permit system",
    "Perform bench calibration to confirm zero and span errors against a certified reference standard",
    "If zero and span cannot both be brought within tolerance, replace sensing element or entire transmitter",
    "Reinstall, perform five-point calibration loop check, and document results on new calibration sheet",
    "Investigate process conditions that may have caused degradation (overpressure, temperature excursions, corrosion)"
  ],
  "rationale": "A zero vs. span error diagnosis from the calibration sheet means the instrument cannot be calibrated so that both the zero point and full-scale span are simultaneously within acceptable tolerance. This is a classic signature of a degraded sensing element or transmitter in service — the element has drifted or been damaged such that the relationship between input and output is no longer linear or stable. Per ISA standards, an in-service instrument that cannot meet calibration tolerances for both zero and span is non-compliant and must be removed from service. The component is degraded (not fully failed, as it still produces a response) but is operating outside acceptable accuracy limits, creating risk of process measurement error.",
  "references": [
    "ISA-51.1 - Process Instrumentation Terminology (definitions of zero error, span error, calibration)",
    "ISA-RP12.06.01 - Recommended Practice for Installation of Intrinsically Safe Systems",
    "ISA standards for instrument calibration and loop accuracy",
    "Site QC calibration procedures and acceptance criteria"
  ]
}
